title: Feature: Hooks-First Architecture Enforcement (ADR-2604012110)
description: Migrate architecture enforcement from hex-agent daemon + hex-nexus REST calls to Claude Code lifecycle hooks. Add hex enforce check-file CLI subcommand. Update settings template with 3 enforcement hooks.

steps.description: Add hex enforce check-file <path> subcommand to hex-cli/src/commands/enforce.rs — reads .hex/adr-rules.toml locally, checks path against forbidden_paths and hex_layer rules, exits 0 (allow) or 1 (block). No daemon required.

Add adr-rules.toml parsing to hex-cli — read forbidden_paths[], hex_layer_map{}, and allowed_patterns[] from .hex/adr-rules.toml (fallback to ~/.hex/adr-rules.toml). Implement as a domain function in hex-core.

Update hex-cli/assets/templates/settings.json (the Claude Code settings template) to include the 3 enforcement hooks: PreToolUse (blocking, check-file), PostToolUse (non-blocking, hex analyze), Stop (blocking, violations-only exit-code).
Generate a default .hex/adr-rules.toml during hex init — populated with standard forbidden_paths (node_modules, .git, dist, .env*) and hex_layer_map from the project's CLAUDE.md architecture rules.
Add hex analyze --file <path> --quiet flag — run treesitter analysis on a single file and suppress output if clean. Exit 0 if no violations, 1 if violations found (for PostToolUse hook integration).
Add hex analyze --violations-only --exit-code flags — run full analysis, print only violations, exit 1 if any found. Used by the Stop hook gate.
Write unit tests for hex enforce check-file: test forbidden path blocking, hex layer validation, allowed patterns, missing rules file graceful degradation (allow when no rules file exists).
Update docs/analysis/hooks-prototype/ settings-snippet.json to match the final hook format from step-3. Add a README section explaining solo vs multi-agent enforcement modes.

phases.name: Analyze flags + Init template
phases.description: Settings template + analyze --file/--violations-only + init default rules

phases.name: Tests + Docs
phases.description: Test coverage and prototype doc update
